name: VCNZ Record-Keeping & Consent
description: Reference example policy aligned with the VCNZ Code of Professional Conduct (NZ).

data.text: This policy applies to every clinical encounter recorded in the practice and reflects the requirements of the Veterinary Council of New Zealand (VCNZ) Code of Professional Conduct.
Records must be clear, accurate, and detailed enough that another veterinarian could continue care.
Patient records are retained for 7 years from the date of the last consultation.
Informed consent is documented for invasive procedures, anaesthesia, and euthanasia, including a record of the specific risks discussed.
When telemedicine is used, the case suitability rationale and informed consent are recorded in the same record.

Schedule 1, 2, and 3 controlled drugs (Misuse of Drugs Act 1975) are recorded in the controlled drugs register, including batch and expiry. Reconciliation occurs at least monthly with two staff present.
Every entry identifies the signing veterinarian; corrections are made by addendum, never by overwrite.
Discharge instructions and follow-up plans are recorded in plain language understood by the client.

clauses.title: Records are clear and complete
clauses.body: You must keep records that are clear, accurate, and detailed enough that another veterinarian could continue care without reinterpretation.

clauses.title: 7-year record retention
clauses.body: You must retain patient records for at least 7 years from the date of the last consultation.

clauses.title: Informed consent recorded
clauses.body: You must document informed consent for invasive procedures, anaesthesia, and euthanasia.

clauses.title: Telemedicine suitability documented
clauses.body: When using telemedicine, you must document the suitability rationale and informed consent.
clauses.source_citation: VCNZ How-to guidance: Using Veterinary Telemedicine

clauses.title: Controlled drugs register kept
clauses.body: You must record Schedule 1, 2, and 3 controlled drug administration in the controlled drugs register and reconcile at least monthly with a witness.

clauses.title: Veterinarian sign-off identified
clauses.body: You must identify the signing veterinarian on every record entry; corrections are by addendum only.

clauses.title: Discharge and follow-up recorded
clauses.body: You should record clear discharge instructions and any follow-up plan in language the client can understand.
